author: śrīla⋅kabikarṇapūrānumoditā, śrīla⋅gopāla⋅bhaṭṭa⋅gosvāminā, śrīla⋅baladeva⋅vidyā⋅bhūṣaṇena coddhṛtā
verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅brahma⋅devarṣi⋅vādarāyaṇa⋅saṁjñakān
śrī⋅madhva⋅śrī⋅padmanābha⋅śrīman⋅nṛhari⋅mādhavān

akṣobhya⋅jayatīrtha⋅śrī⋅jñāna⋅sindhu⋅dayānidhīn
śrī⋅vidyānidhi⋅rājendra⋅jayadharmān⋅kramād⋅vayam

puruṣottama⋅brahmaṇya⋅vyāsatīrthāṁś⋅ca saṁstumaḥ
tato lakṣmī⋅patiṁ⋅śrīman⋅mādhavendrañca bhaktitaḥ

tacchinyān śrī⋅śvarādvaita⋅nityānandān jagad⋅gurūn
devamīśvara⋅śiṣyaṁ śrī⋅caitanyañca bhajāmahe

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅prema⋅dānena jena nistāritaṁ jagat
kali⋅kaluṣa⋅santaptaṁ karuṇā⋅sindhunā svayam

mahā⋅prabhu⋅svarūpa⋅śrīdāmodaraḥ priyaṅkaraḥ,<sup>1</sup>
rūpa⋅sanātanau dvau ca gosvāmi⋅pravarau prabhū
<sup>1</sup><sub><eng>hereafter, jagad⋅guru⋅śrīla⋅bhakti⋅siddhānta⋅sarasvatī⋅ṭhakura is the author</eng></sub>

śrī⋅jīvo raghunāthaśca rūpa⋅priyo mahāmatiḥ
tat⋅priyaḥ kavirājaḥ śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa⋅prabhurmataḥ

tasya priyottamaḥ śrīla sevā⋅paro narottamaḥ
tadanugata⋅bhaktaḥ śrī⋅viśvanāthaḥ saduttamaḥ

tadāsaktaśca gauḍīya⋅vedāntācārya⋅bhūṣaṇam
vidyā⋅bhūṣaṇa⋅pāda⋅śrī⋅baladevaḥ sadāśrayaḥ

vaiṣṇava⋅sārvabhaumaḥ śrī⋅jagannātha⋅prabhustathā
śrī⋅māyāpura⋅dhāmnastu nirdeṣṭā sajjana⋅priyaḥ

śuddha⋅bhakti⋅pracārasya mūlībhūta ihottamaḥ
śrī⋅bhaktivinodo devastat⋅priyatvena viśrutaḥ

tadabhinna⋅suhṛd⋅varyo mahā⋅bhāgavatottamaḥ
śrī⋅gaura⋅kiśoraḥ sākṣād vairāgyaṁ vigrahāśritam

māyāvādi⋅ku⋅siddhānta⋅dhvānta⋅rāśi⋅nirāsakaḥ<sup>2</sup>
viśuddha⋅bhakti⋅siddhāntaiḥ svānta⋅padma⋅vikāśakaḥ
<sup>2</sup><sub><eng>śuddha⋅gauḍīya⋅vaiṣṇavas appended the remaining verses</eng></sub>

devo‛sau paramahaṁso mataḥ śrī⋅gaura⋅kīrtane
pracārācāra⋅kārye nirantaraṁ mahotsukaḥ

hari⋅priya⋅janairgamya oṁ viṣṇupāda⋅pūrvakaḥ
śrī⋅pādo bhakti⋅siddhānta⋅sarasvatī⋅mahodayaḥ

tadantaraṅga⋅varyaḥ śrī⋅bhakti⋅prajñāna⋅keśavaḥ
gaura⋅vāṇī⋅vinode jaḥ kṛtiratneti saṁjñakaḥ

tadantaraṅga⋅varyau dvau matau śrī⋅gaura⋅kīrtane
vāmana⋅nārāyaṇau ca gosvāmi⋅pravarau prabhū

sarve te gaura⋅vaṁśyāśca paramahaṁsa⋅vigrahāḥ
vayañca praṇatā dāsāstaducchiṣṭa⋅grahāgrahāḥ
en_translation:
We offer respects to Śrī Kṛṣṇa, Brahmā, Devarṣi Nārada, and Bādarāyaṇa. We offer respects to Śrī Madhva, Śrī Padmanābha, Śrīmān Nṛhari, and Mādhava.

We offer respects to Akṣobhya, Jayatīrtha, Śrī Jñānasindhu, Dayānidhi, Śrī Vidyānidhi, Rājendra, and Jayadharma in chronological order.

We praise Puruṣottama, Brahmaṇya, and Vyāsatīrtha. Then, with devotion, we praise Lakṣmīpati and Śrīmān Mādhavendra Purī.

We worship their disciples, the Jagad-gurūs Śrī Īśvara Purī, Advaita Ācārya, and Nityānanda Prabhu. We worship the Lord, the disciple of Īśvara Purī, Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu.

He, who is the ocean of mercy Himself, delivered the universe which was burning in the filth of Kali by the gift of Śrī Kṛṣṇa-prema.

Mahāprabhu's Svarūpa Dāmodara is His associate who gives pleasure. Then the two best of the Gosvāmīs, Rūpa and Sanātana.

And Śrī Jīva and Raghunātha, the great-minded one who is dear to Rūpa. His beloved is the great poet Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa Kavirāja.

His dearest friend is the one dedicated to service, Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura. The devotee following him is the always excellent Śrī Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura.

Attached to him is the ornament of the Gauḍīya Vedānta teachers, Śrī Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa, who is our eternal shelter.

Then the Vaiṣṇava-sārvabhauma, Śrī Jagannātha Dāsa Bābājī, who indicated the abode of Śrī Māyāpura and is dear to the saintly people.

The best here, the very root of the preaching of śuddha-bhakti, is the divine Śrī Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, renowned as dear to him.

His non-different best friend, the topmost mahā-bhāgavata, is Śrī Gaura Kiśora Dāsa Bābājī, who is vairāgya personified.

The dispeller of the mass of darkness of the false conclusions of the Māyāvādīs, he causes the lotus of the heart to bloom with the conclusions of viśuddha-bhakti.

He is the Paramahaṁsa, celebrated in the kīrtana of Śrī Gaura, always very eager in the activities of preaching and proper conduct.

Understandable by the people dear to Hari, known as Oṁ Viṣṇupāda Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Ṭhākura Prabhupāda.

His best intimate associate is Śrī Bhakti Prajñāna Keśava, who is known as Kṛtiratna in the pleasure of Gaura-vāṇī.

His two best intimate associates, celebrated in the kīrtana of Śrī Gaura, are Vāmana and Nārāyaṇa, the best of Gosvāmīs and masters.

All of them belong to the lineage of Gaura and are the personifications of Paramahaṁsas. And we are their surrendered servants, eager to take their remnants.
